hypotheses.label: naive's blocking-side corpus saves seeds that carry a well-formed XML document skeleton (the <?xml ...?> + <!DOCTYPE declaration + a real '&...;' reference token) that the parser can advance through to reach xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild, whereas naive_ngram4's saved seeds are malformed surface fragments (URL/attribute garbage, repeated-quote runs) that lack the structural skeleton tokens the deep reference path requires

params.note: STRUCTURAL well-formedness tokens of an XML document head + reference, NOT byte-offset literals. winner_token_count = median over naive resolving seeds of how many distinct skeleton tokens each carries; loser_token_count = same over naive_ngram4 blocking seeds. token_lift = winner_token_count / max(loser_token_count, eps). Per-seed median, so unequal arm sizes do not confound. libxml2 owning server = sB (sB corpora = lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); seeds live in db/blockers.sqlite resolving_seeds/blocking_seeds for this rep.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: the analysis directly observes the seed-composition asymmetry (winner seed offsets 0x1c-0x3f = 'ion="1.0"?>.<!DO' = a clean <?xml...?>+<!DOCTYPE head; loser seed offsets 0x00-0x1f = 'ef="http://fdkeu' = attribute/URL garbage with NO document head). If well-formedness is the mechanism, naive's resolving seeds carry the XML skeleton tokens at a far higher per-seed rate than naive_ngram4's blocking seeds => token_lift >= 2.0 AND winner_token_richer == true. The discriminated ALTERNATIVE this rules out: if the divergence were purely a runtime energy/reach effect with NO seed-composition difference (both arms' saved seeds equally carry the document skeleton, token_lift ~ 1), then the gap would be the depth_reach story or pure stochastic noise -- NOT a well-formedness mechanism. G2-independent: it inspects what bytes/tokens each arm's seeds CONTAIN, never the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: naive's blocking-side corpus actually drives the deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild call chain to the null-parent gate true-side per seed, while naive_ngram4's corpus stays mired in shallow tokenizer states and never reaches it

params.note: winner_fz/loser_fz map to resolving_seeds(naive)/blocking_seeds(naive_ngram4) in db; libxml2 cov image = docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov (owning server = sB)

hypotheses.prediction: G1: when each arm's saved seeds are replayed through the libxml2 cov binary, naive's resolving seeds take the BLOCKED true-side of tree.c:3421 (the analysis observed L3421 T=18) at a far higher per-seed rate than naive_ngram4's blocking seeds (analysis: ngram4 L3421 true=0, false=194) => side_lift >> 1 AND winner_deeper == true. The discriminated ALTERNATIVE: if the divergence were mere stochastic energy noise rather than a structural deep-path-reach gap, both arms' corpora would reach the gate true-side comparably (side_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper == false). G2-independent: depth_reach measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches at the gate line, NOT the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: ngram-4 feedback floods the corpus with near-duplicate shallow-tokenizer seeds, starving energy from the rare deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild path that naive reaches (PRIOR; corpus-SCALE energy story)
hypotheses.prediction: G1: naive_ngram4 corpus is inflated with shallow-gram entries (corpus_size_ratio >> 1) while naive reaches deeper. A non-saturation mechanism predicts comparable corpus sizes.
hypotheses.non_decidable_reason: REFUTED-as-non-discriminable in round 0 and NOT re-tested: corpus_size_ratio measures a whole-campaign corpus-SCALE / energy-allocation property that cannot be attributed to this single branch (it cannot separate ngram saturation-starvation from plain non-mutation of the '&...;' bytes for ONE branch, and n=1 gives no cross-branch threshold support). The per-branch deep-path-reach question this prior was really asking is captured instead by ngram_deep_path_reach_gap (depth_reach) and ngram_wellformed_skeleton_token_gap (token_count). Kept here only as the documented refuted/superseded record.

hypotheses.label: After the two per-branch seed-side tests (token_count seed-composition, depth_reach per-seed gate reach) both scored rule_not_met for libxml2/6939, no remaining BUILT measurement can discriminate this branch's ngram-dilution mechanism: the gate is structural presence/non-null (parent==NULL), with operand_literal==null and no VP/gradient arm, so the two not-yet-tried built tools (byte_freq_ratio, value_distance_reached) are both inapplicable by their own scope_limits. This branch routes to fallback inconclusive rather than being forced a label.

params.rationale: Non-applicability accounting of the two BUILT-but-untried tools flagged as round-3 pivots. (1) operand_enrichment (corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio:target_vs_decoy:per_gate_offset) applies_to fixed-offset VALUE gates and explicitly SKIPS structural/length gates (returns no_gate_signature); this gate is none_structural with operand_literal==null, so there is no target/decoy byte to enrich -- INAPPLICABLE. (2) value_distance_reached (seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch) needs a per-branch operand value (signature.operand_literal) to compute a Hamming/magnitude distance and applies_to vp-gradient families with a vp/vpc arm; operand_literal==null and the decisive pair is naive vs naive_ngram4 (no VP/gradient arm) -- INAPPLICABLE. The only two measurements that COULD discriminate the seed-side mechanism for an ngram structural gate -- seed token composition (token_count) and per-seed deep-path reach (depth_reach) -- are exactly the two already tried and refuted on this branch; a fourth descriptor would be either a relabel of the non-attributable corpus_size_ratio or a relaxed re-test of the two refuted rules, both forbidden.

hypotheses.prediction: Non-discriminable: this is an accounting hypothesis, not a runnable test. ngram-4 is a non-local coverage FEEDBACK (untagged in mutation_op, analogous to value_profile feedback invisibility), and its dilution effect on a STRUCTURAL null-parent gate is only inferable from runtime hit-count ratios -- which the analysis itself flags as its weakest_evidence_point ('inferred from hit-count ratios, not directly observed in corpus-size or energy-allocation telemetry', confidence medium). The two per-branch tests that would have made the seed-side mechanism falsifiable both failed for this branch, and no remaining BUILT descriptor is applicable to a structural operand_literal==null gate on a naive-vs-naive_ngram4 anti pair. Forcing a label would violate G3.
hypotheses.non_decidable_reason: Non-local ngram-feedback family on a STRUCTURAL presence/non-null gate (operand_kind=none_structural, operand_literal=null, no VP/gradient arm). The two applicable per-branch seed-side measurements (token_count, depth_reach) were tried in prior rounds and scored rule_not_met for libxml2/6939. The two BUILT tools not yet tried (byte_freq_ratio, value_distance_reached) are both out of scope by their own scope_limits (byte_freq_ratio skips structural gates; value_distance_reached needs an operand_literal + a vp/vpc arm). corpus_size_ratio remains a non-attributable whole-campaign scale property at n=1. No genuinely-different, falsifiable, applicable, non-redundant BUILT measurement remains -> honest decidable:false; branch routes to fallback inconclusive (G3).

data_realities_respected: Cross-server (rule #4): libxml2 is owned by server B (sB corpora = lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty). The branch is NOT marked decidable:false because its corpus is absent on s4 -- the two decidable:true per-branch tests (token_count, depth_reach) are designed to run on the OWNING server (sB), which has libxml2 corpora + the cov image (docker/targets/Dockerfile.libxml2.cov). They scored rule_not_met on the owning server, which is why the branch routes to inconclusive on the MERITS, not on data absence.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) NOT proposed: its applies_to is fixed-offset VALUE gates and its scope_limits SKIP structural/length gates (returns no_gate_signature). This gate is none_structural (parent==NULL), operand_literal==null -- no target/decoy byte literal exists, so byte_freq_ratio is inapplicable, not merely untried.

value_distance_reached NOT proposed: it needs a per-branch operand value (signature.operand_literal) for the Hamming/magnitude gradient and applies_to vp-gradient families with a vp/vpc arm. operand_literal==null and the decisive pair is naive vs naive_ngram4 (no VP/gradient arm) -- inapplicable, not merely untried.

no seed_lineage 'ngram event' proposed -- ngram-4 is a coverage FEEDBACK, untagged in mutation_op (analogous to the value_profile feedback invisibility); its effect is measured only via what each arm's corpus CONTAINS / REACHES, never via a lineage event.

joint_necessity (seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count:tokens:per_branch) NOT reused as-is: it is scoped to the joint vpc-needs-both ASSEMBLY-DEPTH subtype over a vpc/cmp/vp seed triad -- wrong shape (this is a naive-vs-naive_ngram4 anti pair). Its token-presence idea was already borrowed and re-expressed as the fresh seed_set:token_count descriptor in ngram_wellformed_skeleton_token_gap.
corpus_size_ratio (the prior) remains the genuinely non-attributable whole-campaign scale property; it stays decidable:false and is superseded, not relaxed.
notes: ROUND 3 (FINAL), target=libxml2 (owning server = sB). Shape state: 1 branch (libxml2/6939), 0 confirmed, 1 UNLABELED. SUPERSET/MONOTONIC (rule #1): all 3 priors retained verbatim -- ngram_wellformed_skeleton_token_gap and ngram_deep_path_reach_gap (the KEEP-marked decidable:true tests) and ngram_shallow_corpus_saturation_starves_deep_path (decidable:false, superseded). Per the reinvoke brief both per-branch tests scored rule_not_met for this branch ('2/2 rule(s) scored, none held'): token_count did NOT show the seed-composition asymmetry exceed token_lift>=2.0, and depth_reach did NOT show winner_deeper at side_lift>=2.0. NOVELTY (rule #2): I considered the two BUILT-but-untried pivots the brief flagged (byte_freq_ratio, value_distance_reached) and find BOTH inapplicable to this structural operand_literal==null gate by their own registry scope_limits (byte_freq_ratio skips structural gates; value_distance_reached needs an operand_literal + vp/vpc arm). The only two measurements that COULD discriminate the seed-side ngram-dilution mechanism for a structural gate -- per-seed token composition and per-seed deep-path reach -- are exactly the two already refuted here; a fourth descriptor would be a relabel of the non-attributable corpus_size_ratio or a relaxed re-test of a refuted rule, both forbidden. HONEST G3: I therefore do NOT invent a new mechanism. I add one explicit decidable:false accounting hypothesis (ngram_no_remaining_built_measurement_discriminates) documenting the non-applicability, so the record states WHY no built measurement remains rather than leaving an empty menu. This is the legitimate non-local outcome the brief anticipates: ngram is a non-local coverage feedback, and the analysis itself flags its weakest_evidence_point as 'inferred from hit-count ratios, not directly observed in corpus-size or energy-allocation telemetry' (confidence medium). The branch routes to fallback inconclusive on the MERITS (tests ran on the owning server and the rules did not hold), not on data absence. SUMMARY: kept 3 priors; proposed 0 new runnable mechanisms (no applicable built descriptor remains); 1 new decidable:false accounting entry; branch -> inconclusive.
